annotations.label: Cohomological spectral sequence

anchor.snippet: A cohomological spectral sequence is a sequence

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `CohomologicalSpectralSequence` as a `SpectralSequence` with page-`r` differentials of degree (r, 1-r).

annotations.label: Sheet, page, and differential

anchor.snippet: is called sheet (as in a sheet of paper )

annotations.note: Pages (sheets) are `SpectralSequence.page` (homological complexes) and the differentials d_r are the differentials of those page complexes.

annotations.label: Bigraded spectral sequence

anchor.snippet: every sheet is a bigraded R-module

annotations.note: `SpectralSequence C c r₀` with index type κ = ℤ × ℤ (as used by `CohomologicalSpectralSequence`) is exactly a bigraded spectral sequence.

annotations.label: Complementary degree

anchor.snippet: The notation used here is called complementary degree

annotations.note: This is naming/notation for the second index q (total degree being p+q); there is no dedicated Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Homological spectral sequence

anchor.snippet: one receives the definition of a homological spectral sequence analogously to the cohomological case

annotations.note: The general `SpectralSequence` covers homological grading, and `coreE₂HomologicalNat` builds homological E₂ spectral sequences (differentials of degree (-r, r-1)).

annotations.label: Spectral sequence from a chain complex

anchor.section: Spectral sequence from a chain complex
anchor.snippet: The most elementary example in the ungraded situation is a chain complex

annotations.note: No construction producing a spectral sequence directly from a (single ungraded) chain complex is present.

annotations.label: Morphism of spectral sequences

anchor.snippet: a morphism of spectral sequences

annotations.note: `SpectralSequence.Hom` is a sequence of page morphisms commuting with the homology isomorphisms, making spectral sequences a category.

annotations.label: Multiplicative structure

anchor.snippet: We say it has multiplicative structure if

annotations.note: No notion of a multiplicative/ring structure on spectral sequence pages exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Serre spectral sequence multiplicative structure

anchor.snippet: A typical example is the cohomological Serre spectral sequence for a fibration

annotations.note: The Serre spectral sequence of a fibration is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exact couple

anchor.section: Spectral sequence of an exact couple
anchor.snippet: An exact couple is a pair of objects

annotations.note: Exact couples are not defined in Mathlib; spectral sequences are built from spectral objects instead.

annotations.label: Derived couple

anchor.section: Spectral sequence of an exact couple
anchor.snippet: we will form the derived couple

annotations.note: No derived-couple construction exists since exact couples are absent.

annotations.label: Spectral sequence of a filtered complex

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a filtered complex
anchor.snippet: Then there exists a spectral sequence with

annotations.note: Spectral objects yield spectral sequences (`SpectralObject.spectralSequence`), but there is no construction taking a filtered complex to its spectral sequence.

annotations.label: Double complex

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a double complex
anchor.snippet: A double complex is a collection of objects C i,j for all integers i and j together with two differentials

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex₂ C c₁ c₂` is a bicomplex with two differentials d₁, d₂, i.e. a double complex.

annotations.label: Total complex

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a double complex
anchor.snippet: We define the total complex T ( C •,• ) to be the complex whose n ′ th term is

annotations.note: `HomologicalComplex₂.total` builds the total complex whose degree-n term is the coproduct of the C_{p,q} with p+q=n.

annotations.label: Double complex E0, E1, E2 terms

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a double complex
anchor.snippet: we will work out the E 0 , E 1 , and E 2 terms of the I filtration on T ( C •,• )

annotations.note: There is no spectral sequence of a double complex, so its E0/E1/E2 terms are not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Limiting term

anchor.section: Interpretation as a filtration of cycles and boundaries
anchor.snippet: it is called the limiting term

annotations.note: No E∞ / limiting-term object is defined for spectral sequences in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak convergence

anchor.snippet: We say a spectral sequence converges weakly if

annotations.note: No definition of weak convergence of a spectral sequence is present.

annotations.label: Convergence to a graded object

anchor.snippet: is Hausdorff, i.e.

annotations.note: Convergence of a spectral sequence to a (filtered) graded object is not formalized.

annotations.label: Abutment

anchor.snippet: (the spectral sequence abutment )

annotations.note: The abutment of a spectral sequence is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular / degenerates

anchor.snippet: The spectral sequence is regular or degenerates at

annotations.note: No notion of a regular/degenerating spectral sequence exists.

annotations.label: Collapse

anchor.snippet: then we say it collapses

annotations.note: No notion of a collapsing spectral sequence is defined.

annotations.label: Degeneration for finite filtration

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a filtered complex, continued
anchor.snippet: then the spectral sequence degenerates after the r th sheet

annotations.note: Degeneration after a finite page for a finite filtration is not stated, since convergence theory is absent.

annotations.label: Convergence for bounded filtration

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a filtered complex, continued
anchor.snippet: Convergence also occurs if the complex and the filtration are both bounded below or both bounded above

annotations.note: No convergence criterion for bounded filtered complexes is formalized.

annotations.label: Abutment of filtered complex spectral sequence

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a filtered complex, continued
anchor.snippet: the abutment of the spectral sequence is the p th graded part of the (p+q) th homology of C

annotations.note: The identification of the abutment with the associated graded of the homology of a filtered complex is not formalized.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence from filtered complex

anchor.snippet: we can derive the existence of long exact sequences

annotations.note: The homology long exact sequence of a short exact sequence of complexes (the two-step filtration case) exists, but it is not derived via spectral sequences.

annotations.label: Two gradings are distinct

anchor.section: The spectral sequence of a double complex, continued
anchor.snippet: In general, the two gradings on

annotations.note: This is an informal remark comparing the two double-complex filtrations on total homology; not a formal statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutativity of Tor

anchor.snippet: However, it turns out that

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Tor` and `Tor'` (deriving in each factor) but explicitly leaves the isomorphism `Tor C n ≅ Tor' C n` unproven.

annotations.label: First-quadrant spectral sequence

anchor.snippet: this is called a first-quadrant spectral sequence

annotations.note: `CohomologicalSpectralSequenceNat` has pages indexed by ℕ × ℕ, i.e. a first-quadrant spectral sequence.

annotations.label: First-quadrant sequence abuts

anchor.snippet: The sequence abuts because

annotations.note: `IsFirstQuadrant` is the vanishing condition giving a (convergent) first-quadrant spectral sequence, but the abutment/convergence theorem itself is not proven.

annotations.label: Two non-zero adjacent columns

anchor.snippet: for all p other than 0, 1

annotations.note: The two-column collapse computation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Two non-zero adjacent rows

anchor.snippet: whose second page consists only of two lines q = 0, 1

annotations.note: The two-row (Gysin-type) collapse computation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Wang sequence

anchor.snippet: Consider a fibration over a sphere

annotations.note: The Wang exact sequence for a fibration over a sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: Five-term exact sequence

anchor.snippet: we get the so-called five-term exact sequence

annotations.note: The five-term exact sequence of low-degree terms is not formalized.

annotations.label: Edge maps (homological)

anchor.snippet: They are called the edge maps

annotations.note: Edge maps of a homological spectral sequence are not defined.

annotations.label: Transgression (homological)

anchor.snippet: The transgression is a partially defined map

annotations.note: The (homological) transgression map is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge maps (cohomological)

anchor.snippet: then there is a sequence of epimorphisms

annotations.note: Edge maps of a cohomological spectral sequence are not defined.

annotations.label: Transgression (cohomological)

anchor.snippet: The transgression is a not necessarily well-defined map

annotations.note: The (cohomological) transgression map is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transgression determines differential

anchor.snippet: the transgression map determines the differential

annotations.note: No statement relating the transgression to spectral sequence differentials exists, since transgression is absent.
